pr.title: Port Timeline tab into Timeweave with docs-driven TimeWeave UI and routing
pr.body:
This PR ports the Timeline tab capability into `timeweave` by introducing the missing app/docs structure and wiring a Timeline route that renders timeline content via `TimeWeave`. It also brings in the supporting hook/sidebar/plugin integration required for the tab to function end-to-end in this repository.

- **Timeline docs route + plugin wiring**
  - Added Docusaurus configuration to mount a dedicated docs plugin at `/timeline`.
  - Added `sidebars-timeline.js` and navbar entry bound to the `timeline` docs plugin/sidebar.
  - Redirected the root page to `/timeline` for direct entry into the feature.

- **Timeline UI implementation**
  - Added `src/components/TimeWeave.jsx` and `src/components/TimeWeave.module.css`.
  - Added `src/hooks/useContentItems.js` to read timeline docs metadata from Docusaurus global docs data and sort entries by date.

- **Timeline content integration**
  - Added `docs/timeline/index.mdx` that renders `@site/src/components/TimeWeave`.
  - Added initial timeline content docs under `docs/timeline/*.mdx` with frontmatter (`title`, `description`, `date`) consumed by the component.

- **Project baseline required to host the feature**
  - Added minimal Docusaurus project files (`package.json`, `docusaurus.config.js`, `src/css/custom.css`, `.gitignore`) and updated `README.md` for repository usage.

```mdx
---
title: Timeline
---

import TimeWeave from '@site/src/components/TimeWeave';

<TimeWeave />
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

Port the entire Timeline tab feature from `MatthewCDoyle/athenaeum` into `MatthewCDoyle/timeweave`.

Source repository: `MatthewCDoyle/athenaeum`
Target repository: `MatthewCDoyle/timeweave`
Base branch: `main`

Goal:
- Add the full Timeline tab implementation from Athenaeum into Timeweave.
- Create a pull request with the required code and content changes.

Known source files and feature pieces from Athenaeum:
- `src/components/TimeWeave.jsx`
- `src/components/TimeWeave.module.css`
- `docs/timeline/index.mdx`
- `docs/timeline/*.mdx` content files
- `sidebars-timeline.js`

Important implementation details:
- `docs/timeline/index.mdx` renders `@site/src/components/TimeWeave`.
- `src/components/TimeWeave.jsx` depends on `../hooks/useContentItems`, so the agent must inspect Athenaeum for any required hook(s), utilities, MDX/content integration, Docusaurus docs plugin configuration, navbar links, sidebar config, and any other dependencies needed to make the Timeline tab actually work in Timeweave.
- Do not copy build artifacts or `.docusaurus` generated files unless absolutely necessary; prefer source files and configuration.
- Inspect the current structure of `MatthewCDoyle/timeweave` and integrate cleanly rather than blindly copying files.
- If Timeweave is currently minimal or empty, scaffold the necessary source structure to support the Timeline tab.
- Ensure the Timeline route is available and wired up in the target repo.
- Preserve functionality and styling as closely as practical.

Expected outcome:
- A PR against `main` in `MatthewCDoyle/timeweave` that adds the Timeline tab feature and its required dependencies from Athenaeum.
- The PR description should summarize what was copied/adapted and call out any assumptions or follow-up work if applicable.

The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat exploration (may be truncated):


- So the ‚Äúentire Timeline tab‚Äù is not just that MDX page ‚Äî it also depends on:
  - `src/components/TimeWeave`

  - `docs/timeline/*` content files
  - `sidebars-timeline.js`

I also found code-search results in Athenaeum, but code search is capped and may be incomplete. To view more in GitHub UI, use:
`https://github.com/search?q=Timeline+repo%3AMatthewCDoyle%2Fathenaeum&type=code`

The blocker:

- Without access to the target repo, I can‚Äôt map where the Timeline feature should be inserted or write the files safely.

**Next step:** please provide one of these:
1. access to `MatthewCDoyle/timeweave`, or
2. the correct Timeweave repo URL if that repo name is different, or
3. a request to create a PR explicitly, if you want me to open one once access works.

Once I can read `timeweave`, I‚Äôll port the Timeline tab end-to-end rather than just copying the top-level MDX file.

Assistant: I can proceed, but I still need one thing from you: **explicit permission to create a PR** or permission to write directly to `MatthewCDoyle/timeweave`.

Right now I‚Äôve confirmed:
- `MatthewCDoyle/timeweave` exists and its default branch is `main`.
- The Athenaeum Timeline tab is built from:
  - `src/components/TimeWeave.jsx`
  - `src/components/TimeWeave.module.css`

However, I still haven‚Äôt been able to read `timeweave`‚Äôs contents through the contents endpoint, so I don‚Äôt want to blindly push files into a repo layout I can‚Äôt inspect.

**Fastest path:** reply with either:
- **‚ÄúCreate a PR in MatthewCDoyle/timeweave‚Äù**  
or
- **‚ÄúWrite directly to MatthewCDoyle/timeweave main‚Äù**

If you want, I can also prepare the exact file set I‚Äôll copy:

- `src/components/TimeWeave.module.css`
- `docs/timeline/index.mdx`
- all `docs/timeline/*.mdx` files from Athenaeum

One important note: Athenaeum‚Äôs `TimeWeave.jsx` depends on `../hooks/useContentItems`, so Timeweave may also need that hook and any related content plumbing copied or adapted. Without inspecting `timeweave`, that dependency is the biggest integration risk.
